meanings.usage: 指根据现有信息推断未来事件
指提前知道或预见将要发生的事

meanings.meaning: 众所周知地；声名狼藉地
meanings.usage: 修饰形容词，强调某事出名（通常指坏的方面）
指大型计算机系统，通常用于企业或政府机构
形容早期、技术不发达的事物
指容易使用和理解的产品或系统
指结合文字、图像、声音等多种媒介的技术
指拒绝接受或不认真考虑某个想法
与of连用，表示具备做某事的能力
主要用于医学领域，指判断疾病
指不再稀奇、变得普通的事物
表示即时发生，没有延迟
形容值得注意或不同寻常的事物
常与of连用，表示免除某人的负担或工作
指涉及电子技术的设备或系统

phrases.usage: 早在...（用于强调时间久远）

examples.en: As long ago as the 1960s, Leon Bagrit predicted these uses.
examples.zh: 早在20世纪60年代，莱昂·巴格瑞特就预测到了这些用途。

examples.en: It pointed the way to the future.
Many of their functions have been taken over by small personal computers.
examples.zh: 许多功能已被小型个人电脑所取代。

examples.en: They would be capable of providing information about traffic jams.
examples.zh: 它们将能够提供有关交通拥堵的信息。

phrases.usage: 解除某人的...；使某人从...中解脱

examples.en: They would relieve office workers of dull, repetitive clerical work.
examples.zh: 它们将把办公室工作人员从枯燥重复的文书工作中解放出来。

phrases.usage: 他也无法预见（用于否定句后加强语气）

examples.en: Nor could he have foreseen the development of the Internet.
examples.zh: 他也不可能预见互联网的发展。
与电话网络相连的计算机。

examples.en: Computers have become more and more powerful.
examples.zh: 计算机变得越来越强大。

phrases.phrase: it is even more remarkable that

examples.en: It is even more remarkable that an Englishman could predict these uses.
examples.zh: 更值得注意的是一个英国人能够预测这些用途。

examples.en: Bagrit dismissed the idea that computers would learn to 'think' for themselves.
examples.zh: 巴格瑞特摒弃了计算机会学会'独立思考'的观念。

grammar.title: 情态动词 + have + 过去分词（表达对过去的推测或可能性）
grammar.definition: 情态动词could/might/must + have + 过去分词结构用于表达对过去事件的推测、批评或惊讶
grammar.usage: 这个结构用于：1) 对过去的推测（could have been, might have been）；2) 虚拟语气中的假设；3) 表达'本可以'但未实现的可能性

examples.en: Who could have imagined, in the mid 1970s, that computers would be common in homes?
examples.zh: 在20世纪70年代中期，有谁能够想象计算机会在家庭中普及？

examples.en: Leon Bagrit couldn't possibly have foreseen the development of the Internet.
examples.zh: 莱昂·巴格瑞特不可能预见互联网的发展。

examples.en: If he were alive today, he might be able to tell us what to expect.
examples.zh: 如果他今天还活着，他也许能告诉我们该期待什么。

grammar.title: nor/neither...nor 的倒装用法
grammar.definition: 当nor或neither...nor连接两个否定成分时，可以使用倒装语序
grammar.structure: Nor/Neither + 助动词/be动词/情态动词 + 主语 + ... .
grammar.usage: 用于否定句后，连接第二个否定成分，强调两者都不。nor could he have foreseen是'他也无法预见'的意思，强调连...也无法做到

examples.en: Nor could he have foreseen the development of the Internet.
examples.zh: 他也不可能预见互联网的发展。

examples.en: Neither does he want to go, nor will he change his mind.
examples.zh: 他既不想去，也不会改变主意。

grammar.title: 过去完成时在间接引语中的使用
grammar.definition: 当主句动词是过去时，间接引语中的动词通常需要使用过去完成时
grammar.structure: 主句动词（过去时）+ that + 主语 + had + 过去分词
grammar.usage: 用于报道过去某人所说的话或想法，使时间关系更加清晰。Bagrit foresaw a time when computers would be small...中，would表示过去的将来

examples.en: Bagrit dismissed the idea that computers would learn to 'think' for themselves.
examples.zh: 巴格瑞特摒弃了计算机会学会'独立思考'的观念。

examples.en: He predicted that computers would be small enough to hold in the hand.
examples.zh: 他预测计算机会小到可以握在手中。

original.en: Who could have imagined, in the mid 1970s, that by the end of the 20th century, computers would be as common in people's homes as TV sets?
original.zh: 在20世纪70年代中期，有谁能够想象到20世纪末计算机会像电视机一样普及到家庭中？

imitations.en: Who could have imagined, in the 1990s, that smartphones would replace cameras?
imitations.zh: 在90年代，有谁能够想象智能手机会取代相机？

imitations.en: Who could have predicted that AI would develop so rapidly?
imitations.zh: 有谁能够预测人工智能会发展得如此迅速？

original.en: Of course, Leon Bagrit couldn't possibly have foreseen the development of the Internet.
original.zh: 当然，莱昂·巴格瑞特不可能预见互联网的发展。

imitations.en: The ancient Egyptians couldn't possibly have foreseen modern technology.
imitations.zh: 古埃及人不可能预见现代科技。

imitations.en: Early scientists couldn't possibly have imagined the internet.
imitations.zh: 早期的科学家不可能想象到互联网。

sentencePatterns.pattern: It is even more remarkable that...

original.en: Considering how recent these developments are, it is even more remarkable that as long ago as the 1960s, an Englishman was able to predict some of these uses.
original.zh: 考虑到这些发展是多么新近，尤其值得注意的是，早在20世纪60年代，一个英国人就能预测到其中一些用途。

imitations.en: It is even more remarkable that he solved the problem in minutes.
imitations.zh: 更值得注意的是他在几分钟内就解决了这个问题。

imitations.en: It is even more remarkable that she learned three languages in a year.
imitations.zh: 更值得注意的是她在一年内学会了三种语言。

sentencePatterns.pattern: ...when they would be capable of doing...

original.en: Bagrit foresaw a time when computers would be small enough to hold in the hand, when they would be capable of providing information about traffic jams.
original.zh: 巴格瑞特预见了这样一个时代：计算机会小到可以握在手中，能够提供交通拥堵信息。

imitations.en: Scientists foresee a time when robots would be capable of doing all housework.
imitations.zh: 科学家预见了机器人能够做所有家务的时代。

imitations.en: He imagined a future when people would be capable of traveling to Mars.
imitations.zh: 他想象人们能够前往火星的未来。
